Sexually Transmitted Infections Treatment Guidelines, 2021 
Error processing SSI file 
Mycoplasma genitalium 
Print 
Minus 
Related Pages 
M. genitalium causes symptomatic and asymptomatic urethritis among men and is the etiology of approximately 15%–20% of NGU, 20%–25% of nonchlamydial NGU, and 40% of persistent or recurrent urethritis ( 697 , 909 , 910 ). Infection with C. trachomatis is common in selected geographic areas ( 911 – 913 ), although M. genitalium is often the sole pathogen. Data are insufficient to implicate M. genitalium infection with chronic complications among men (e.g., epididymitis, prostatitis, or infertility). The consequences of asymptomatic infection with M. genitalium among men are unkno
